title: Add resumable human interaction plane
summary: Browser foreground human handoff and the optional agent-device 0.19.3 iOS Simulator provider are implemented, locally merged, and verified on final main. Browser and iOS failures remain capability-local; physical devices, automated CAPTCHA solving, nested MCP, arbitrary CLI execution, and tokenized deep links remain outside the capability boundary. Direct-edit tasks are verified but cannot auto-complete because the current task lifecycle requires Agent Run integration/cleanup evidence.
goals: Browser sessions can be presented in the foreground and remain open for manual CAPTCHA/login completion.
Human handoff state is durable and resumable across plugin actions and Worker lifetimes.
Existing browser actions refuse conflicting profile use while a handoff host owns the profile.
The existing iOS plugin gains an optional, version-pinned agent-device simulator provider with typed actions and local evidence.
Browser and iOS failures remain capability-local and never degrade global Controller readiness.
nonGoals: No payment, purchase, account deletion, or other irreversible remote action.
No physical iPhone mutation support in this change.
No nested MCP server and no Controller dependency on agent-device.
acceptanceCriteria: Focused browser tests cover foreground presentation, durable handoff state, resume, stale-host recovery, and profile conflict fencing.
Focused iOS tests cover absent dependency degradation, exact booted simulator selection, command construction, artifact registration, and close-on-failure.
Typecheck and runtime architecture checks pass.
Changes are isolated, committed, merged locally, and leave the repository clean.

tasks.title: Implement browser human handoff host
tasks.objective: Add a provider-neutral interaction session store and a detached browser handoff host. Expose present_session, request_human_handoff, get_handoff_status, resume_session, and cancel_handoff through the browser plugin. Preserve the existing one-action Playwright model outside handoff, fence profile conflicts, use best-effort macOS foreground activation, capture final URL/title, and clean stale hosts safely.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser stays open while waiting for the user and closes only after resume/cancel/timeout.
Subsequent automated actions use the updated persistent profile after resume.
No ordinary browser action can launch the same profile during an active handoff.
notes: RUN-iss-20260719-f77e4c-t1-1784450076049-3879ff59 ended as unknown; explicit retry is required. worker did not start before the startup deadline
Executor failed before startup and produced no work; discard the empty isolated run and continue with supervised direct edit.

Discard stale T1 run: executor never started, task is cancelled, worktree no longer exists, and there is no diff or unique work to preserve. Clearing obsolete drift-prevention ownership so T7 can launch from current main.

tasks.title: Add optional agent-device simulator provider
tasks.objective: Integrate the proven agent-device PoC as an optional provider inside the existing iOS plugin. Pin version 0.19.3, reject physical devices, require exact uniquely booted simulator names, expose doctor/open/snapshot/press/fill/scroll/screenshot/events/close actions, keep artifacts local/controller-owned, and close sessions on failure. Do not make agent-device required for existing iOS readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing Xcode/simulator capability remains ready when agent-device is absent.
Physical devices and ambiguous simulator selection are rejected before any app action.
Every mutating UI action is serialized by resource claims and records bounded evidence.
notes: Superseded because it depends on cancelled T1. A replacement task will target the verified T3 interaction contract and current main.

tasks.title: Add optional agent-device simulator provider on verified interaction plane
tasks.objective: Integrate the existing agent-device PoC as an optional, version-pinned simulator provider under the current iOS plugin and verified interaction-session architecture. Pin 0.19.3, reject physical devices, require an exact uniquely booted simulator, expose bounded doctor/open/snapshot/press/fill/scroll/screenshot/events/close actions, register local artifacts, serialize mutating UI actions, close sessions on failure, and preserve existing Xcode/simulator readiness when the dependency is absent. Do not start a nested MCP server or alter global Controller readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing Xcode and simulator capabilities remain ready when agent-device is absent.
Physical devices and ambiguous or non-booted simulator selection are rejected before any app action.
The provider is pinned to agent-device 0.19.3 and executes only typed local CLI actions without a nested MCP server.
Mutating UI actions are serialized, artifacts remain local and bounded, and sessions close on failure.
Focused iOS tests, typecheck, runtime architecture, and controller-v8 pass on final main.
notes: Superseded because direct-edit T3 is verified but cannot become dependency-terminal without synthetic Agent Run cleanup evidence. Replacement execution task will use verified main revision f7afe0e5 as its baseline without a false lifecycle dependency.

tasks.title: Add bounded physical iOS device interaction provider
tasks.objective: Extend the verified iOS plugin and provider-neutral interaction-session architecture with an optional physical-device provider for paired iPhones. Reuse CoreDevice/devicectl for discovery, installed-app lookup, launch and process lifecycle. Establish a signed XCTest/WebDriverAgent-style runner only when required for UI automation. Expose exact-device open/snapshot/press/fill/scroll/screenshot/events/close actions without weakening the existing simulator provider. Support third-party App Store apps such as JD where platform APIs permit it. Keep credentials, pairing records and signing material outside repository state. Serialize mutating actions, redact sensitive fields, require manual handoff for passwords, verification codes, biometric prompts, purchases and payments, and fail closed when screen capture or accessibility control is unavailable. Do not jailbreak, bypass iOS security controls, automate payment confirmation, or claim capabilities not proven on the paired device.
acceptanceCriteria: The iOS plugin lists paired physical devices separately from simulators and reports exact readiness and limitations.
The provider can open an exact paired device session and launch an installed third-party app by bundle identifier without accepting ambiguous device names.
When a supported signed automation runner is available, snapshot, press, non-sensitive fill, scroll and screenshot operate through one serialized interaction session and produce bounded evidence.
When the runner, screen capture, developer mode, trust or signing prerequisites are unavailable, actions fail with actionable typed errors rather than silently falling back or claiming success.
Passwords, verification codes, biometrics, purchasing, checkout submission and payment remain human-only and are blocked or handed off explicitly.
Existing simulator agent-device behavior and optional dependency readiness remain backward compatible.
Focused tests and declared package checks pass, and one live smoke test proves at least physical-device discovery, installed-app lookup and JD launch on the paired iPhone.
